The “3x + 1” conjecture, better known as the Collatz conjecture, works like this:

If

Repeat the process. The conjecture says every positive integer eventually reaches 1.

items.gemini: It looks like you're diving into the $3x + 1$ Conjecture, more famously known as the Collatz Conjecture. Despite its simple rules, it’s one of the most notorious

in mathematics because no one has been able to prove it works for every single number!The rules are straightforward:If the number is even, divide it by 2: $ rac{x}{2}$If the number is odd, multiply by 3 and add 1: $3x + 1$Repeat until you reach the number 1.Here is the verification for your specific integers:a) 7The number 7 is notorious for taking a bit of a scenic route before finally hitting 1.7 is odd $ightarrow (3 	imes 7) + 1 = \mathbf{22}$22 is even $ightarrow 22 / 2 = \mathbf{11}$11 is odd $ightarrow (3 	imes 11) + 1 = \mathbf{34}$34 is even $ightarrow 34 / 2 = \mathbf{17}$17 is odd $ightarrow 40 / 2 = \mathbf{20}$20 is even $ightarrow 20 / 2 = \mathbf{10}$10 is even $ightarrow 10 / 2 = \mathbf{5}$5 is odd $ightarrow (3 	imes 5) + 1 = \mathbf{16}$16 $ightarrow$ 8 odd $ightarrow (3 	imes 53) + 1 = \mathbf{160}$160 is even $ightarrow 160 / 2 = \mathbf{8 0}$80 is even $ightarrow 80 / 2 = \mathbf{40}$40 $ightarrow$ 20 $ightarrow$ 10 $ightarrow$ 5
